message:
fix: eliminate false-positive HTTP liveness probe timeouts (#74)

Three root causes conspired to produce spurious WARN-level "HTTP liveness
probe failed" logs even while /healthz returned 200 and the server was
fully responsive:

1. **Per-probe HttpClient creation**: `probe_http_healthz()` built a new
   HttpClient on every call with `idle_timeout(Duration::ZERO)`, forcing
   a fresh TCP connection each time.  Under even light load the TCP
   handshake alone could exceed the timeout.

   Fix: Extract `build_probe_http_client()` and reuse a single client
   across all probes within a supervisor generation.  The client is
   rebuilt after each server restart to avoid stale connections.

2. **Probe failures counted without cross-referencing server diagnostics**:
   The supervisor loop incremented `liveness_failures` on every probe
   timeout, even when `HttpRequestRuntimeDiagnostics.last_completed_at_ms`
   showed the server had successfully completed a real HTTP request within
   the probe interval window.

   Fix: Before incrementing `liveness_failures`, check whether the
   server's own diagnostics show a successful request completion within
   the last `http_probe_interval_secs` window.  If so, demote the log
   from WARN to DEBUG and skip the failure counter entirely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
